BONNEY , AN INDIVIDUAL's DEMURRER to 1ST Amended CROSS COMPLAINT Transferred to department 302 to be heard on September 17, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. per the order of the presiding judge. = (501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ARTURO OSIEL VALENCIA BARRERA VS. CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco?S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Declare Plaintiff A Vexatious Litigant And Request For Prefiling Order And Order Requiring Plaintiff To Furnish Security
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 03, 2024, line 2. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Declare Plaintiff A Vexatious Litigant And Request For Prefiling Order And Order Requiring Plaintiff To Furnish Security. GRANTED as follows: 1. Defendant's Motion to Declare Plaintiff a Vexatious Litigant is GRANTED. Under California Code of Civil Procedure (CCP) 391(b), a vexatious litigant is defined as a pro per plaintiff who filed at least five litigations within the last seven years which have been finally determined adversely to them. Plaintiff has filed in-excess of the minimum five required cases adversely decided against him within the past seven years to meet the standard of a vexatious litigant. 2. Defendant's request for an order requiring Plaintiff to furnish security in the amount of $25,000 is GRANTED. Upon finding that the Plaintiff is a vexatious litigant, the Court shall order the vexatious litigant to furnish security in the amount it deems appropriate per CCP 391.3. 3. Defendant's request for a prefiling order under CCP 391.7(a) is GRANTED. To file any future pro per litigations, Plaintiff must first obtain leave from the presiding judge. Violation of this order may be punishable by contempt of court. 4. The Court takes judicial notice of Defendant's 82 Exhibits in support of their motion. The Court may take judicial notice of a document's legal effect and where there is only one reasonable interpretation of a document. The Court takes judicial notice of the existence of prior litigation filed by the Plaintiff and the adverse results. = (501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TOY REAL ESTATE INVESTMENT COMPANY VS. MARSHALL WEXLER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Set Aside The Judgment Entered Into On June 14
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 03, 2024, line 4. DEFENDANT MARSHALL WEXLER's Motion To Set Aside The Judgment Entered Into On June 14. DENIED. Moving party fails to set forth any legal or factual grounds to set aside a judgment. The motion lacks Notice of Motion and Motion. The Motion to Set Aide appears to be an opposition to the request for entry of judgment. The Court notes that according to the proof of service this motion, signed on July 16, 2024, was served by mail and e-mail on July 1, 2024. = (501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
